tree.setup: A Chinese language classroom with green chalkboards and morning light slanting through tall windows. Teacher Wang writes every character on the board as though she's painting a scroll. She once spent four minutes perfecting a single 我 while the class watched in reverent silence. Today she's returning homework.
tree.setup_zh: 中文教室，绿色黑板，早晨的阳光从大窗户照进来。王老师在黑板上写每一个字都像在画画。她曾经花了四分钟写一个「我」字，全班安静地看着。今天她在发作业。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你好！你的作业写得很好。
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ hǎo! nǐ de zuòyè xiě de hěn hǎo.
turns.text_en: Hello! Your homework is written very well.

options.feedback: Teacher Wang gives a small, satisfied nod. In Chinese classrooms, 老师 (lǎoshī) is both a title and a term of respect -- always use it when addressing your teacher.
Teacher Wang smiles at your enthusiasm. 太好了 is a wonderfully versatile expression of happiness.
Teacher Wang tilts her head. She just complimented you -- a simple 谢谢 would have been the natural response here.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ měi tiān dōu xiě hànzì ma?
turns.text_en: Do you write Chinese characters every day?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her about your study habits.

options.text_pinyin: shì de, wǒ měi tiān xiě.
options.feedback: Teacher Wang's eyes light up. She pulls out a practice sheet from her desk -- a gift for the dedicated student. 每天 (měi tiān, every day) is a key time phrase.
Teacher Wang nods slowly, in the way that means she's already planning additional practice sheets for you. 有时候 is honest and natural.
Teacher Wang inhales as though you've said you don't like breathing. She will recover, but it may take a moment. 不喜欢 is grammatically correct, though.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese education, beautiful handwriting (书法, shūfǎ) is deeply valued. Teachers often comment on the quality of character writing, not just correctness. 老师 is used as a respectful form of address -- never use a teacher's first name alone.
